title: Six Winners of the 2024 Innovation Fund Hydrogen Auction Sign Grant Agreements
content: Six Winners of the 2024 Innovation Fund hydrogen auction have signed their grant agreements, receiving a total of â‚¬270.6 million sourced from EU Emissions Trading System revenues, supporting renewable hydrogen rollout in the EU and helping scale up European hydrogen production under the domestic pillar of the European Hydrogen Bank. Two projects were awarded under the maritime topic with more than â‚¬35 million to supply renewable hydrogen to maritime off takers, while the funding aims to bridge the difference between production costs and the price industrial users are willing to pay, reducing risks for investors and easing administrative burdens. The renewable fuel of non biological origin hydrogen is expected to be used in sectors like chemical industry and mobility, helping decarbonise maritime and heavy duty transport and projected to produce around 500 kilotonnes of renewable hydrogen, cutting almost 3.4 million tonnes of CO2 emissions over ten years. The projects in Spain, Finland and Norway will install 381.25 megawatts of electrolyser capacity with grant values ranging from â‚¬135.5 million to â‚¬1.8 million and bids from â‚¬0.33 to â‚¬1.88 per kilogram.
author: Analysts Account
